title: project-context.json
description: Structure + intent snapshot of a TypeScript/JavaScript project, produced by an independent extractor and consumed by AI workflow generators as ADVISORY context (not a runtime-enforced contract). Mirrors the design philosophy of CC Workflow Studio's workflow JSON: stable ids, a discriminating `kind`, deterministic structural fields kept separate from natural-language intent, and a top-level metadata block.

schemaVersion.description: Semver of this file format. Lets consumers migrate. PoC: 0.1.0.

description.description: Human/AI-readable note about what this file is for.

root.description: Repo root the paths are relative to (usually ".").

summary.description: MEANING LAYER (LLM-generated, project level). 1-3 lines: what this project is and how it is structured. Lets the generation prompt drop 'this project is ...' boilerplate.

generatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the (manual, full) regeneration.

structureFingerprint.description: OPTIONAL / FUTURE. Single hash over the structure layer (paths + exports + kinds). Used only as a staleness *signal* (warn 'structure changed since intent was written'). NOT used for diff/partial updates in the PoC — full manual regeneration only.

files.description: One entry per source file. Analogous to workflow `nodes`. The PoC deliberately omits inter-file edges (the analogue of workflow `connections`) — a flat catalogue, not a call graph.

id.description: Stable id (e.g. "f-001"). Stable across regenerations for the same path so consumers/diffs can reference a file by id.

kind.description: STRUCTURE LAYER (heuristic). Best-effort classification from path/filename/extension. Advisory only — wrong guesses are acceptable in the PoC.

exports.description: STRUCTURE LAYER (deterministic, via ts-morph). Names of top-level exported symbols (functions, classes, consts, types, interfaces, enums). The 'where is X defined' index that saves the agent a grep.

intent.description: MEANING LAYER (LLM-generated, file level). One line: what this file is FOR / its responsibility. Optional so a 'structure-only' run can omit it (experiment Pattern A).

intentStatus.description: Freshness of `intent`. 'missing' = never generated; 'stale' = structure changed since intent was written (future staleness signal). Advisory.
